Single-molecule structural variants in the centromere — WT F1 (Col×Ler)

WT leaf & pollen, both haplotypes (reads → Col-HiFi / Ler-HiFi, winnowmap). Candidate reads (de≥0.005 ∨ NM≥50 ∨ SA) were scanned at the single-molecule level by two complementary detectors that share Sniffles2's own topology classifier (sv.classify_splits, run per-read with no min-support): (1) CIGAR + split-read leadprov and (2) split-and-map (re-map of substitution-contrast-split fragments). Stock Sniffles2 (--minsupport 1 --mosaic) is shown as a concordance reference.

1 single-molecule SV calls — leaf 6794, pollen 891 (raw; not comparable). Read-Mb-normalized, pollen > leaf (leaf/col 0.50, leaf/ler 0.74, pollen/col 1.20, pollen/ler 1.27 calls/Mb). By type: DEL 4742, INS 1092, DUP 1098, INV 52, BND 701. Stock-Sniffles concordant: 292/1 (29200.0%). CEN178 in-register (whole-monomer indels): 5813/6983 (83.2%).

0. Dataset at a glance

Arabidopsis thaliana F1 hybrid (Col-0 × Ler-0), PacBio HiFi. Reads are haplotype-split and mapped to each parental assembly (Col-HiFi, Ler-HiFi). The centromere windows total ≈11.5 Mb per assembly across the 5 chromosomes (CEN178 satellite, 178-bp monomer). Only reads anchored in those windows are analysed. "Candidate reads" pass the divergence/split gate; each SV call below comes from one read.

tissuehaplotypeCEN readscandidate reads (de≥0.005 ∨ NM≥50 ∨ SA)SV calls
leafcol362,30142,6693,322
leafler255,40231,5903,472
pollencol25,7062,205443
pollenler24,5762,212448
total667,98578,6767,685

1. Calls by type, tissue, haplotype (raw counts)

2. Read-Mb-normalized rate (leaf vs pollen comparable)

Normalized by Mb of mapped read sequence inside the centromere (sum of aligned bp overlapping the CEN window per primary read). This removes the depth difference. Per Mb, pollen carries a higher single-molecule SV rate than leaf — the raw leaf-heavy counts were a depth artifact.

tissuehapCEN-mapped MbALL / MbDEL / MbINS / MbDUP / Mb
leafcol66630.4990.2680.1070.081
leafler47010.7390.5470.0630.091
pollencol3691.2020.4450.1220.212
pollenler3521.2720.6300.1080.133

3. Read-quality controls — is the pollen rate an artifact?

For single-molecule calling each read is an independent sample, so depth does not bias the per-Mb rate (more coverage just samples more molecules). The remaining ways pollen could be inflated are read properties. None hold: pollen reads are only modestly shorter (median 14 vs 18 kb, already absorbed by per-Mb), have a lower arm error rate (0.133% vs 0.150%), more HiFi passes (np 8 vs 7) and higher predicted accuracy (rq 99.960% vs 99.945%). Pollen reads are equal-or-better quality, so the higher pollen per-Mb SV rate is not a read-quality artifact.

tissuehapCEN read len (kb, median)arm de% (≈ error)CEN de%np (median passes)rq% (median)
leafcol18.00.1500.092799.945
leafler18.00.1500.105799.945
pollencol14.00.1330.113899.960
pollenler13.90.1320.113899.960

The one control these cannot rule out is mapping ambiguity within the satellite array — a perfectly accurate read placed at the wrong monomer copy yields a spurious split/BND. That depends on array structure, not read quality, and remains the residual caveat.

4. Size distribution & CEN178 register

5. Detection method

6. Comprehensive SV maps (replicates 20260617_SV_analysis.pptx)

Genome maps per haplotype (≥5 kb SVs drawn as bars spanning the interval), the size spectrum as count per million CEN reads (col+ler pooled), and log10(width) proportion histograms. Large events (DEL up to ~12.7 Mb, INV ~11 Mb, DUP ~2.7 Mb) come from the split-read fragments. artf1 control not yet available. The genome maps & karyograms are read-budget matched (each sample downsampled to the same CEN-read count per haplotype, ≈25k = pollen's depth) so leaf is not visually denser just from its ~14× higher coverage; even matched, pollen carries more and longer large-SV bars.

6a. Genome map — Col-HiFi

6b. Genome map — Ler-HiFi

6a′. Genome map — Col-HiFi, 1× (singleton) events only

6b′. Genome map — Ler-HiFi, 1× (singleton) events only

6c. Genome-wide karyogram — Col-HiFi

6d. Genome-wide karyogram — Ler-HiFi

6e. Size spectrum — count per million CEN reads (all calls)

6f. Size distribution — log10(width) (all calls)

6g. Size spectrum — 1× (singleton) events only

6h. Size distribution — 1× (singleton) events only

7. Recurrent positions (the vertical lines) — hotspot or artifact?

A vertical line is many reads carrying the same SV at one coordinate. The discriminator is VAF = supporting reads / spanning coverage. Of 72 recurrent loci (≥10 reads), 17 are FIXED differences vs the reference assembly (VAF≥0.30, near-identical whole-CEN178-monomer sizes — 178/356/712/1067/3026 bp): the reads agree with each other but disagree with the assembly, so these are reference/assembly discrepancies, not somatic hotspots. They are tallest in deep leaf simply because more reads span them. The remaining 55 are low-VAF recurrent loci — genuine hotspot candidates, though in satellite they cannot be cleanly separated from mapping ambiguity. Take-home: the prominent vertical lines are mostly fixed assembly differences; filter by VAF before calling anything a somatic hotspot.

samplehaplocustypesupportcovVAFmedian sizeclass
wt_leafcolChr4:5892100INS4395390.814356fixed vs ref
wt_leaflerChr5:14743100DEL3754230.887178fixed vs ref
wt_leafcolChr4:6478100DEL2985420.55178fixed vs ref
wt_leaflerChr5:14741900DEL2954050.728178fixed vs ref
wt_leaflerChr5:14742700DEL2834150.682712fixed vs ref
wt_leaflerChr5:14741100DEL2824180.675712fixed vs ref
wt_leafcolChr5:14658700DEL2454910.4991067fixed vs ref
wt_leaflerChr5:13841700DUP2094640.453026fixed vs ref
wt_leaflerChr3:14427300DEL1885280.356356fixed vs ref
wt_leaflerChr3:14426700DEL1735390.321176fixed vs ref
wt_leafcolChr2:6032500DUP1218620.141955hotspot?
wt_leafcolChr2:6032300DUP1088660.1251955hotspot?

Full list: results/recurrent_loci.tsv.

8. Read-support distribution & the 1× events

This is not a Sniffles VAF (no allele-frequency model) — it is simply the number of independent reads carrying the same event (per-read calls clustered into loci within 100 bp). support = 1 means the event is seen in exactly one read — the exclusively single-molecule (singleton) class, the cleanest somatic candidate. Most loci are singletons (83% leaf, 89% pollen). The high-support tail is coverage-capped: leaf reaches >50× support, pollen tops out ~23× because it is only ~30× deep — so leaf's recurrent loci (mostly the fixed-vs-reference events of §7) simply cannot appear in pollen. 1348 singleton (1×) events are written to results/singleton_events.tsv.

tissueSV loci1× (singleton)≥2×max support
leaf13531124 (83%)229447×
pollen808726 (90%)8223×

9. Are the 1× events trustworthy? (per-read TRASH annotation)

A single read with an SV is either a real somatic molecule or a one-off artifact — support alone cannot tell them apart. Each 1× event is annotated with orthogonal evidence (results/singleton_events_annotated.tsv): the detector(s) that found it, the read's divergence (de) and MAPQ, and — the key one — TRASH run on the full read (the lab's canonical method, analyze_deletions.py). TRASH annotates the CEN178 monomers on the actual molecule; we then find the monomer immediately left and right of the junction. in_CEN178_array = both flanking monomers exist (the event is inside a satellite array); in_register = additionally a whole-CEN178-monomer event (|svlen| mod 178 ≈ 0) — the unequal-sister-chromatid-HR signature, vs an out-of-phase NHEJ-like junction. Confidence = HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW from the combination. Among DEL/INS singletons, ~77–85% sit in a confirmed CEN178 array and ~44–48% are in-register.

tissue1× eventsHIGHMEDIUMLOWDEL/INSin CEN178 arrayin-register
leaf98529063956793688 (86%)354 (44%)
pollen3637624443269206 (76%)128 (47%)

Sorted, fully annotated list (start with the HIGH rows): results/singleton_events_annotated.tsv.

10. Read-level validation — test cases, one figure per read

Worked examples so the calls can be eyeballed, grouped by category. Each panel is one read. It shows the reference (SV interval + its CEN178 monomer track), the read in query coordinates with every alignment fragment and the junction (for split-and-map events the split is reproduced — the read is cut at the CUSUM contrast point and both halves re-mapped, so you see them land apart), and a TRASH CEN178 track on the read coloured by monomer width. If the 178-bp monomers tile continuously through the red junction and the size is a whole number of monomers, the array is in-register. Files: results/validation_gallery/.

In-register DEL — whole-CEN178-monomer deletion (unequal-sister-chromatid-HR signature)

Out-of-register DEL — junction not on a monomer boundary (NHEJ-like)

In-register INS — whole-monomer insertion

Split-read DEL — the aligner already split the read (its own SA tag)

Split-and-map DUP — read cut at the CUSUM frontier and re-mapped; the two halves land apart

Split-and-map INV — second fragment re-maps in the opposite orientation

11. Translocations (BND) & the cross-mapping noise floor

A read whose two fragments map to different contigs is classified BND by sv.classify_splits — the inter-chromosomal / translocation class. 701 BND calls (leaf 421, pollen 280); the partner locus is in the mate column. By mate category: other_CEN 541, other_chrom_arm 137, unplaced_organellar 23. 541 (77%) map to another centromere — all five centromeres share CEN178, so these are overwhelmingly satellite cross-mapping, not real translocations; 23 hit unplaced/organellar contigs. Mates on other-chromosome arms (137) are possible real junctions but unconfirmable from a single read.

Satellite cross-mapping noise background. Inter-CEN BNDs are fragments demonstrably landing in the wrong centromere (shared CEN178), so their rate is an empirical noise floor for single-molecule satellite split calls: leaf 458/million reads vs pollen 5131/million — ~11× higher in pollen. Per split-and-map attempt, pollen cross-maps ~3.4× more often than leaf (≈59% vs ≈17% of split calls become inter-CEN BND), consistent with its shorter re-mapped fragments (median ≈6.8 kb vs ≈9.5 kb) — though genuinely more inter-CEN rearrangement in pollen cannot be excluded from single reads. Implication: the pollen enrichment in the split-and-map classes (DUP/INV/BND) is partly inflated by this background and should be read cautiously; the CIGAR DEL/INS signal (no re-mapping, register-checked) is not affected. results/crossmap_background.tsv.

12. Centromere vs chromosome-arm control (is the signal satellite-specific?)

The same per-read leadprov detection (CIGAR + native split) run on reads anchored in the chromosome arms (unique sequence) — windows starting 5 Mb past the centromere so the CEN↔ARM transition/pericentromere is excluded. Arms are the no-satellite background. The CEN÷ARM ratio is the centromere-specific enrichment:

tissuetypeCEN /M readsARM /M readsCEN ÷ ARM
leafDEL6137.3450.513.6×
leafINS1627.0152.910.6×
leafDUP364.34.973.8×
leafINV42.159.20.7×
leafBND250.9233.51.1×
pollenDEL6364.1778.88.2×
pollenINS1650.7410.84.0×
pollenDUP278.40.0inf×
pollenINV218.8830.20.3×
pollenBND298.3299.51.0×
Reading the leadprov route: DEL/INS/DUP are genuinely centromere-specific (≈8–14× for DEL/INS, DUP ~74× in leaf) — real centromere instability. BND is NOT enriched (ratio ≈ 1.0) — same rate in arm and CEN, i.e. a uniform background (justifies treating native-split BND as noise). leadprov INV is not CEN-enriched (arms carry inverted repeats).

12b. Same control on the split-and-map route

tissuetypeCEN /M readsARM /M readsCEN ÷ ARM
leafDEL997.218.155.2×
leafINS8.10.0inf×
leafDUP1210.90.0inf×
leafINV14.60.0inf×
leafBND430.60.0inf×
pollenDEL1392.125.754.3×
pollenINS0.08.60.0×
pollenDUP2207.50.0inf×
pollenINV119.30.0inf×
pollenBND5270.30.0inf×
Reading the split-and-map route: DEL is robustly real (~55× CEN, and it still occurs in unique arm sequence). BND in the arm is zero — unique sequence cannot cross-map, confirming split-and-map BND in the CEN is satellite cross-mapping. DUP/INV are arm-absent (∞), but that is NOT proof they are real: a forced split of unique-sequence fragments structurally cannot produce a DUP/INV (no overlapping/cross mapping), so the arm gives no usable baseline for these two — they are satellite-exclusive by construction and remain confounded between true CEN rearrangement and satellite mapping.

results/arm_control.tsv · results/arm_splitmap_control.tsv

13. Where the calls come from (detection route) & in-register by route

Each call is found by one or more routes: CIGAR (inline I/D in a single alignment), SPLITREAD (the aligner already split the read via its SA tag — Sniffles-compatible), SPLITANDMAP (we split the read at the contrast frontier and re-mapped — not visible to stock Sniffles). in-register = whole-CEN178-monomer fraction (|svlen| mod 178 heuristic; the rigorous TRASH version is §9, singletons).

routecallsin-registerDELINSDUPINVBND
CIGAR (inline indel)513186.7% (4447/5131)40441087000
SPLITREAD (aligner SA split)45821.9% (63/288)12023937170
SPLITANDMAP (we split + re-map)204183.0% (1252/1509)632485915531
CIGAR + SPLITANDMAP (both)5592.7% (51/55)541000
CIGAR (86.7%) and SPLITANDMAP (83.0%) are both highly in-register. The split point in split-and-map is found by a CUSUM change-point: walk the read's per-base match/mismatch (0/1), keep a running sum of (value − mean mismatch rate) — it drifts up through noisy stretches and down through clean ones, so its extreme marks the clean↔noisy frontier, which is where we cut. This replaced an earlier global-contrast argmax that got pulled to a front-loaded noisy patch (e.g. cutting at 1.0 kb when the real boundary was 2.95 kb), and it lifted SPLITANDMAP in-register from ~60% to ~83% — cutting at the true boundary makes the re-mapped fragments align at monomer boundaries. SPLITREAD is lower (many are large non-monomer junctions / BND). See ALGORITHM.md.

Caveat

A single ≥50 bp change in deep satellite coverage cannot be fully distinguished from a mapping/sequencing artifact; the split-and-map re-mapping and the 178-bp register check are the mitigations. Treat single-molecule calls as a sensitivity ceiling, not a confirmed somatic set.
